questions.question: If 2p = [(√9 + √8)/(√9 - √8)] + [(√9 - √8)/(√9 + √8)], then which of the following is true?
If 3x/y=√16, then find the value of (x²+y²)/xy + y²/(xy+x²).
questions.explanation: 3x/y=√16 Then, x=4y/3 Now, (x²+y²)/xy + y²/(xy+x²) = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p5720/image003.png) = 101/42

questions.question: Find the sum of the unit digit of ((22 x 33)¹⁷ + (14 x 18)²²)?
questions.explanation: The concept of cyclicity is used to identify the last digit of the number. Cyclicity chart: NumberCyclicityUnit digit values Power 1Power 2Power 3Power 4 010 111 242486 343971 4246 515 616 747931 848426 9291 (Note: when remainder is 0, power = cyclicity) In (22 x 33)¹⁷ = 726¹⁷ Unit digit of 726 is 6. From the cyclicity chart, cyclicity of 6 is 1. So, we have to divide the power of 726 i.e.17 by 1. After dividing 17 by 1, remainder = 0. So, power = cyclicity = 1 Unit digit of 6¹ = 6 In (14 x 18)²² = 252²² Unit digit of 252 is 2. From the cyclicity chart, cyclicity of 2 is 4. So, we have to divide the power of 252 i.e. 22 by 4. After dividing 22 by 4, remainder = 2. So, power ≠ cyclicity In this case, unit digit of 2² = 4 Therefore, sum unit digit of ((22 x 33)¹⁷ + (14 x 18)²²) = 6 + 4 = 10

questions.question: In a shelter of hens and cows, the total number of legs is 180 and the total number of heads is 65. What is the difference between the number of hens and cows?
questions.explanation: Let the number of hens and cows be 'a' and 'b' respectively. So, a + b = 65..(i) and, 2a + 4b = 180..(ii) (ii) - (i)*2 2b = 50 => b = 25 a = 65 - 25 = 40 So, required difference = 40 - 25 = 15

questions.question: The average of present ages of X and Y is 45 years. If the ratio of their ages 6 years from now will be 7:10, what is the ratio of their present ages?
questions.explanation: Let the present age of X = 'a' years Present age of Y = 45*2 - a = (90 - a) years So, (a + 6)/(90 - a + 6) = 7/10 10a + 60 = 672 - 7a 17a = 612 a = 36 Ratio of their present ages = 36 : (90 - 36) = 2:3

questions.question: What is the value of (1 - cos⁶ 2A - sin⁶ 2A)/(cos 3A + cos A)²?
questions.explanation: 1 - cos⁶ 2A - sin⁶ 2A = 1 - [(cos² 2A)³ + (sin² 2A)³] = 1 - [(cos² 2A + sin² 2A)³ - 3 sin² 2A cos² 2A(cos² 2A + sin² 2A)] = 1 - [1 - 3 sin² 2A cos² 2A] = 3 sin² 2A cos² 2A = 12 sin² A cos² A cos² 2A (cos 3A + cos A)² = 4 cos² 2A cos² A So, (1 - cos⁶ 2A - sin⁶ 2A)/(cos 3A + cos A)² = (12 sin² A cos² A cos² 2A)/(4 cos² 2A cos² A) = 3 sin² A

questions.question: How many terms are there in the series 1/0.01 + 1/0.001 + 1/0.0001 +⋯ = 11111100 ?
questions.explanation: Given series is in the form of G.P.: Then, first term = a = (1/0.01) Common ratio = r = (1/0.001)/(1/0.01) = 10 Let total terms = n Sum = a(rⁿ - 1)/(r - 1) = 11111100 (1/0.01)(10ⁿ - 1)/(10 - 1) = 11111100 10ⁿ = 10⁶ n = 6 terms

questions.question: If α and β are roots of the equationx²+7x+2=0, then what is the equation whose roots are α²β⁴ and β²α⁴?
questions.explanation: Given equation: x²+7x+2=0 Here, αβ = 2 and (α + β) = -7 Now, (α²β⁴)(β²α⁴) = (2)⁶ = 64 And, (α²β⁴ + β²α⁴) = α²β²(α² + β²) = α²β²((α + β)² - 2αβ) = (2)²((-7)² - (2)(2)) = 180 Therefore, the new equation: x²-(α² β⁴ + β² α⁴ )x+ (α² β⁴)(β² α⁴)=0 x²-180x+64=0

questions.question: If M = (8/3) * (⅙) * (9/5) + (7/4) ÷ (5/3) and N = (16/37) * (⅜) + (5/4) * (⅔), then what is the value of M * N?
What is the difference between total number of cars crossing bridge on Monday and Friday taken together and total number of cars and buses crosses the bridge on Thursday?
questions.explanation: DayBusCar Monday560680 Tuesday640480 Wednesday600520 Thursday420620 Friday680440 Total number of cars crosses bridge on Monday and Friday taken together = 680 + 440 = 1120 Total number of cars and buses crosses the bridge on Thursday = 420 + 620 = 1040 Therefore, difference = 1120 - 1040 = 80

questions.question: What is the respective ratio of total number of cars crosses the bridge on Tuesday to the total number of buses crosses the bridge on Wednesday?
questions.explanation: DayBusCar Monday560680 Tuesday640480 Wednesday600520 Thursday420620 Friday680440 Total number of cars crosses the bridge on Tuesday = 480 Total number of buses crosses the bridge on Wednesday = 600 Ratio = 480: 600 = 4: 5

questions.question: What is the average of total number of cars crosses the bridge from Monday to Friday taken together?
questions.explanation: DayBusCar Monday560680 Tuesday640480 Wednesday600520 Thursday420620 Friday680440 Average of total number of cars crosses the bridge from Monday to Friday taken together = (680 + 480 + 520 + 620 + 440)/5 = 548

questions.question: Total number of buses crosses the bridge on Tuesday and Wednesday taken together are approximately what percent of total number of cars crosses the bridge on same days taken together?
questions.explanation: DayBusCar Monday560680 Tuesday640480 Wednesday600520 Thursday420620 Friday680440 Total number of buses crosses the bridge on Tuesday and Wednesday taken together = 640 + 600 = 1240 Total number of cars crosses the bridge on Tuesday and Wednesday taken together = 480 + 520 = 1000 Therefore, percentage = (1240/1000) x 100 = 124%

questions.question: If 615a6 is divisible by 4, then find the sum of all possible values for 'a'.
questions.explanation: If 615a6 is divisible by 4, then the last two digits of the given number will be divisible by 4. Then, a6 is divisible by 4. Then, the two digit numbers formed by 'a6' which are divisible by 4 = 16, 36, 56, 76, 96 So, the possible values of 'a' = 1, 3, 5, 7, 9 Therefore, the sum of all possible values of 'a' = 1 + 3 + 5 + 7 + 9 = 25

questions.question: A batsman scores 99 runs in the 33ᵗʰ match of his career. How much average runs per match increased so that 66 runs is his average runs per match before 33ʳᵈ match?
questions.explanation: Average of batsman after 32 matches = 66 Let x runs per match increased So, 66 * 32 + 99 = 33(66 + x) => 2211 = 2178 + 33x => 2211 - 2178 = 33x => x = 33/33 => x = 1 Alternate solution x = (99 - 66)/33 = 1

questions.question: Which of the following is true? I. ⁶√83 > ⁴√17 > √3 II. ⁴√17 > ⁶√83 > √3 III. ⁶√83 > √3 >⁴√17 IV. √3 > ⁶√83 > ⁴√17

Small cylinders of base radius 3.5 cm and height 5 cm are kept inside a cuboidal box of dimensions 28 cm * 14 cm * 15 cm in such a way that base of cylinders is parallel to the base of box. If maximum number of cylinders are placed inside the box, then how much volume of box remain void?
questions.explanation: Total number of layers that can be kept in the box = Height of cuboid/Height of one cylinder = 15/5 = 3 Diameter of cylinder = 2 * 3.5 = 7 cm Total rows of cylinder that can be kept along the length of box= 28/7 = 4 Total rows of cylinder that can be kept along the width of box= 14/7 = 2 Total number of cylinders that can be kept inside box = 3 * 4 * 2 = 24 Volume of 24 cylinders = 24 * (22/7) * 3.5² * 5 = 4620 cm³ Volume of cuboid = 28 * 14 * 15 = 5880 cm³ Volume of void = 5880 - 4620 = 1260 cm³

questions.question: Ajay, Kumar and Sonu entered into a partnership. Ajay invested ¼ᵗʰ of the money for ⅙ᵗʰ of the time, Kumar invested ⅓ʳᵈ of the money for ¼ᵗʰ of the time and Sonu invested rest of the money for rest of the time. Sonu being the working partner, 10% of the profit goes to him. If they earned a total profit of Rs.159000. Find the share of Kumar in the profit.
questions.explanation: Ratio of share in the profit: Ajay : Kumar : Sonu = (¼ x ⅙) : (⅓ x ¼) : (1 - ¼ - ⅓) x (1 - ⅙ - ¼) = 1/24 : 1/12 : (5/12 x 7/12) = 1/24 : 1/12 : 35/144 = 6 : 12 : 35 Amount from which profit is to be divided = (100 - 10)% of 159000 = 143100 Share of Kumar in the profit = 12/(6 + 12 + 35) x 143100 = 12/53 x 143100 = Rs.32400

questions.question: The distance between two cities X and Y is 234 km. A train starts at 5 a.m. from X moving towards Y at 72 km/hr. Another train leaves at 6 a.m. from Y and starts running at 90 km/hr towards X. At what time do the two trains meet?
questions.explanation: Let the two trains meet after x hours after 5 a.m. Distance travelled by train starting from X = 72 X x Distance travelled by train starting from Y = 90 X (x - 1) ∴ 72 X x + 90 X (x - 1) = 234 ∴ 72x + 90x - 90 = 234 ∴ 162x = 324 ∴ x = 2 So, the trains meet at (5 + 2) i.e. 7 a.m. Hence, option c is correct.

questions.question: Current population of a village is 15300. What was the population of the village 2 years ago if population has decreased during those 2 years at the rate of 10% per annum and 15% per annum respectively?
questions.explanation: If the population has decreased at 10% and 15% respectively then the overall decrease is = -10-15+ (10 X 15)/100= -25+1.5= -23.5% Let the current population be p. Given: p-23.5/100 p=15300 76.5/100 p=15300 →p= (1000 X 15300)/765=20000

questions.question: Find the value of (sec A sin A)/( tan A + cot A) =?
questions.explanation: (sec A sin A)/( tan A + cot A) = ((1/ cos A) sin A)/ (sin A/cos A + cos A/sin A) = (sin A/cos A)/sin²A + cos² A/ sin A cos A = (sin A/cos A)/ 1/sin A cos A = sin²A cos A / cos A = sin²A

questions.question: A certain sum is invested for certain time. It amounts to Rs 100 at 10% per annum. But when invested at 6% per annum, it amounts to Rs 76.find the sum.
questions.explanation: A certain sum is invested for certain time. It amounts to Rs X at P% per annum. But when invested at Q% per annum, it amounts to Rs Y. Then the sum is = (YP-XQ) / (P-Q) Here X=Rs 100, Y=Rs 76, P= 10%, Q=6% So the sum is = (76*10 - 100*6) / (10-6) Sum = (760-600) / 4= 160/4 = Rs 40

questions.question: 16 men and 12 children can complete a work in 26 days. 13 men can complete that work in 48 days. In how many days will 12 men and 6 children complete that work?
questions.explanation: Given, 13 men can complete that work in 48 days and the same work is done by 16 men and 12 children in 26 days. So, (16M + 12C) x 26 = 13M x 48 On solving we get 2M = 3C.....(1) Also it is asked that in how many days will 12 men and 6 children complete that work. 6 children = 4 men ( from 1) So 12 men and 6 children = 12 + 4 = 16 men Let the no. of days in which 12 men and 6 children will complete that work be 'a' According to the question: 13M x 48 = 16M x a a = 39 days

questions.question: Two chords AB and CD of a circle intersect at E such that AE = 3.4 cm, BE = 4.2 cm and CE = 2.6 cm. What is the approximate length of DE?
questions.explanation: The Intersecting Chord Theorem states that when two chords intersect each other inside a circle, the products of their segments are equal. => AE * BE = CE * DE => 3.4 * 4.2 = 2.6 * DE => DE = 5.49 ≈ 5.5

questions.question: Sanju gave 20% discount on an item but on the customer's request he gave an additional discount of 10% on the item. What is the tag price of the item if the customer purchased it at Rs.1026?
questions.explanation: Let the marked price be M. M x 0.8 x 0.9 = 1026 => 0.72M = 1026 => M = Rs 1425
